interactions.summary: Jiang says Russia also benefits from the Middle East war because it helps Russia in Ukraine and oil profits can support Iran against America and Israel.
Tucker asks whether the American president has the authority to constrain Israel as a client state.

Jiang says Israel no longer behaves rationally because it is overtaken by eschatological fever: secular losses, even nuclear war, matter less than faith, divine intervention, and messianic redemption.
Tucker asks how eschatological extremism arose among American Protestants, Israeli Jews, and American Jews after a period when political Islam became less central.

Jiang says eschatology deeply influences American politics through Christian Zionism and long-running religious or secret-society traditions aimed at Israel, the Third Temple, Gog and Magog, and messianic age scenarios.

claims.claim: Jiang says Russia benefits from the Middle East war because it diverts America, raises oil profits, and can help finance Iranian resistance.
Jiang argues Israel's political behavior is being shaped by eschatological fever: extreme danger is read as the condition under which God will intervene and send the Messiah.
claims.temporal_scope: Interpretive claim about Israeli religious politics as of 2026-03-20.

claims.claim: Jiang says Christian Zionism is a powerful American political force, with groups such as Christians United for Israel helping fund West Bank settlements and Middle East conflict politics.
claims.temporal_scope: American political diagnosis in the 2026-03-20 interview.

claims.claim: Jiang claims current geopolitical events are converging with an eschatological script involving Israel, a Third Temple, Al-Aqsa, Gog and Magog, and messianic redemption.

signature_moments.moment: Secular catastrophe loses weight when politics is possessed by a messianic script.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the packet's bridge from geopolitics to eschatology as a governing force.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang warns that eschatology cannot be treated as fringe because it has mass political organization in America.
signature_moments.source_phrase: we can never overestimate the influence of eschatology in American politics
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It makes the religious layer operational rather than decorative.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A condition in which political actors treat catastrophe as part of a divine end-time scenario rather than as a secular cost to avoid.
An American evangelical movement Jiang describes as treating Israel as the linchpin of God's plan and the return of Jesus.

chronology_notes.note: This interview gives a developed 2026 eschatology reading of Israel, American evangelicals, and Third Temple politics.

uncertainty_notes.note: Jiang presents some claims as rumors or behind-the-scenes speculation; later public use should preserve that uncertainty.
